Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a set of activities and we need to construct a DECLARE model for the scenario described The DECLARE model in pm4py is expressed as a Python dictionary with keys for the constraints

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We have two types of constraints For keys existence absence exactly one init the value is a dictionary where the keys are the activities and the value is another dictionary with support and confidence For the other set of keys the value is a dictionary where the keys are pairs of activities and the value is a dictionary with support and confidence

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However for binary constraints we need two activities So I think there might be a misinterpretation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at the official pm4py documentation for DECLARE models The DECLARE model is a dictionary where each key is a constraint template and the value is of the form activity one activity two with support and confidence for binary constraints For unary constraints the key is just the activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Given the above I will construct the model as follows For unary constraints the value is a dictionary where each key is an activity string and the value is a dictionary with support and confidence For binary constraints the value is a dictionary where each key is a tuple of two activities and the value is a dictionary with support and confidence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We are not given any specific rules so we have to make up a set of rules that make sense for the process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets think about the process We start with Idea Generation so we might have an init constraint for IG Then we do Design Draft after IG We have Technical Feasibility Check and Cost Evaluation after DD and before Prototype Creation Then we do Prototype Creation followed by Laboratory Testing and User Testing Then we have Approval Gate after testing and then Marketing Plan and Final Launch

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets try to set up some constraints The process must start with IG After IG we must do DD After DD we must do TFC and CE these two can be in any order but both must occur after DD and before PC After TFC and CE we can do PC After PC we must do LT and UT these two can be in any order but both must occur after PC and before AG After LT and UT we must do AG After AG we must do MP After MP we must do FL

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Given that in a single case we can assume each activity occurs exactly once in a case Lets set exactly one for each activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We set init for IG exactly one for each activity response for IG to DD AG to MP and MP to FL precedence for DD to TFC DD to CE TFC to PC CE to PC PC to LT PC to UT LT to AG and UT to AG

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: We can also set chainresponse for IG to DD and maybe MP to FL if we are sure that MP is immediately followed by FL

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: We cannot set chainprecedence for DD to TFC or DD to CE because there might be the other activity in between So we do not set those chain constraints

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: We can also set nonsuccession for FL to other activities because FL is the last activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Well use the following structure model init exactly one response precedence chainresponse nonsuccession We are not given specific support and confidence so we set them to 1 0
